::: {.chapter-num}
Chapter IV
:::

## Where things shift {.chapter-title}

::: {.chapter-lede}
Two ways to ask "when did something change?" — first, the moments where one tracker's typical level seems to step up or down; then, a single number per day for how unusual the whole day was.
:::

::: {.analysis-section}

### Change points

A statistical method scans your HRV, Resting HR, and Stability Score timelines and looks for moments where the typical value clearly shifted up or down — not just for a day or two, but settled into a new baseline.
The dashed lines mark those moments, with the date next to each.
Pair them with your memory: did anything change in your life around those dates?
A new medication, an infection, a season change, a stressful stretch?
The number of shifts considered scales with how long you've been logging — more years, more permitted breakpoints.
If there are no lines, your baseline has stayed roughly steady — also useful to know.

```{webr}
#| label: chart-change-points
#| input: ["fileText", "darkMode"]

if (exists("visible") && requireNamespace("changepoint", quietly = TRUE)) {
  viz <- viz_theme(darkMode)

  series <- visible |>
    filter(tracker %in% c("HR Variability", "Resting HR",
                                 "Stability Score")) |>
    group_by(tracker, odate) |>
    summarise(value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
                     .groups = "drop") |>
    arrange(tracker, odate)

  tracker_range <- aggregate(value ~ tracker, series,
                              function(x) c(min = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
                                            max = max(x, na.rm = TRUE)))
  tracker_range <- data.frame(tracker = tracker_range$tracker,
                               vmin = tracker_range$value[, 1],
                               vmax = tracker_range$value[, 2])

  cp_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(split(series, series$tracker),
    function(df) {
      ok <- !is.na(df$value)
      if (sum(ok) < 20) return(NULL)
      cp <- suppressWarnings(
        changepoint::cpt.mean(df$value[ok],
                              method = "BinSeg", Q = cp_Q,
                              penalty = "BIC")
      )
      idx <- changepoint::cpts(cp)
      if (length(idx) == 0) return(NULL)
      dts <- df$odate[ok][idx]
      data.frame(tracker = df$tracker[1],
                 odate = dts,
                 label = format(dts, "%d %b %Y"))
    }))

  if (!is.null(cp_df) && nrow(cp_df) > 0) {
    cp_df <- merge(cp_df, tracker_range, by = "tracker", all.x = TRUE)
  }

  p <- ggplot(series, aes(odate, value)) +
    geom_point(colour = viz$muted, alpha = 0.45, size = 0.9) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x, se = FALSE,
                colour = viz$accent, linewidth = 0.9) +
    facet_wrap(~ tracker, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
         title = "Moments your baseline shifted",
         subtitle = sprintf(
           "Dashed lines mark dates where the typical level moved up or down · up to %d per tracker",
           cp_Q)) +
    viz$theme(base_size = 12)

  if (!is.null(cp_df) && nrow(cp_df) > 0) {
    p <- p +
      geom_vline(data = cp_df,
                 aes(xintercept = odate),
                 colour = viz$secondary,
                 linetype = "dashed",
                 linewidth = 0.5) +
      geom_label(data = cp_df,
                 aes(x = odate, y = vmin, label = label),
                 colour = viz$secondary,
                 fill = viz$paper_card,
                 label.size = 0,
                 label.padding = grid::unit(0.12, "lines"),
                 hjust = -0.05, vjust = 0,
                 size = 2.8,
                 fontface = "italic")
  }
  p
} else if (exists("visible")) {
  cat("changepoint package not available in this WebR build — skipping.\n")
}
```

:::

::: {.analysis-section}

### Daily symptom burden

This boils every day down to a single number: across all your symptom trackers, how unusual was today compared to your own typical day?
**Zero is your usual day.**
Positive values mean a worse-than-usual day across multiple trackers at once; negative values mean an easier-than-usual one.
The thin line is each individual day (jumpy by nature); the thick curve smooths over the noise to show the underlying drift.
A long stretch above zero is a flare; a slow tilt downward over months is recovery; bouncing tightly around zero is stability.

```{webr}
#| label: fact-burden
#| input: ["fileText"]

if (exists("visible_mat_clean") && ncol(visible_mat_clean) >= 5) {
  mat <- visible_mat_clean
  mu <- colMeans(mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  sigma <- apply(mat, 2, stats::sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  sigma[sigma == 0] <- 1
  z <- sweep(sweep(mat, 2, mu, "-"), 2, sigma, "/")
  burden_per_day <- rowMeans(z, na.rm = TRUE)
  dates <- as.Date(rownames(mat))

  worst_idx <- which.max(burden_per_day)
  best_idx <- which.min(burden_per_day)

  htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
    paste0(
      '<p class="section-fact">',
      'Your hardest day on this combined measure: <strong>%s</strong> (%+.1f, well above your usual). ',
      'Your easiest: <strong>%s</strong> (%+.1f, well below). ',
      'Calculated across <strong>%d</strong> symptom trackers — the more trackers you log, the more reliable this single number becomes.',
      '</p>'
    ),
    format(dates[worst_idx], "%b %d %Y"),
    burden_per_day[worst_idx],
    format(dates[best_idx], "%b %d %Y"),
    burden_per_day[best_idx],
    ncol(mat)
  ))
}
```

```{webr}
#| label: chart-burden
#| input: ["fileText", "darkMode"]

if (exists("visible_mat_clean") && ncol(visible_mat_clean) >= 5) {
  viz <- viz_theme(darkMode)

  mat <- visible_mat_clean
  mu <- colMeans(mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  sigma <- apply(mat, 2, stats::sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  sigma[sigma == 0] <- 1
  z <- sweep(sweep(mat, 2, mu, "-"), 2, sigma, "/")

  burden <- data.frame(
    odate = as.Date(rownames(mat)),
    burden = rowMeans(z, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_logged = rowSums(!is.na(mat))
  )
  burden <- burden[burden$n_logged >= 5, ]

  worst <- burden[which.max(burden$burden), ]
  best  <- burden[which.min(burden$burden), ]

  ggplot(burden, aes(odate, burden)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = viz$rule, linewidth = 0.4) +
    year_marks(burden$odate, viz, is_multi_year) +
    geom_point(colour = viz$ink, alpha = 0.4, size = 0.9) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x, se = TRUE,
                colour = viz$accent, fill = viz$accent_soft,
                linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(data = worst,
               colour = viz$accent, fill = "white",
               shape = 21, size = 3.2, stroke = 1.2) +
    geom_text(data = worst,
              aes(label = sprintf("hardest: %s", format(odate, "%d %b %Y"))),
              vjust = -1, hjust = 0.5,
              colour = viz$accent, size = 3.2, fontface = "italic") +
    geom_point(data = best,
               colour = viz$secondary, fill = "white",
               shape = 21, size = 3.2, stroke = 1.2) +
    geom_text(data = best,
              aes(label = sprintf("easiest: %s", format(odate, "%d %b %Y"))),
              vjust = 2, hjust = 0.5,
              colour = viz$secondary, size = 3.2, fontface = "italic") +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    labs(x = NULL,
         y = "How unusual was today?",
         title = "Daily symptom burden over time",
         subtitle = if (is_multi_year)
           "Above zero = worse than usual · curve shows the trend · dotted lines mark new years"
         else
           "Above zero = worse-than-usual day · below = better · the curve shows the underlying trend") +
    viz$theme(base_size = 13)
}
```

:::
